title: Fix video call feature bugs

requirements.text: Audit and fix all bugs in the video call feature end-to-end: the video call button in both FriendListItem (Friends panel) and PrivateChatView header must correctly open the VideoCallModal; the useVideoCall hook must successfully request and initialise the local camera/microphone media stream without errors; mute (audio) and camera toggle controls must reflect the correct state and actually mute/unmute or enable/disable the corresponding media track; ending the call must stop all media tracks, release the stream, and close/unmount the VideoCallModal cleanly; and the simulated 'Calling…' → 'Connected' state transition must work as intended. Ensure no unhandled promise rejections or console errors occur during the full call lifecycle.

quotes: The video call feature isn't working

acceptanceCriteria: Clicking the video call button on a friend list item opens the VideoCallModal without errors.
Clicking the video call button in the private chat header opens the VideoCallModal without errors.
The local camera stream initialises successfully and the local video feed is visible in the picture-in-picture area.
The mute button toggles the audio track on/off and its icon/label reflects the current muted state.
The camera toggle button enables/disables the video track and its icon/label reflects the current camera-off state.
The simulated 'Calling…' state transitions to 'Connected' after the expected delay.
Clicking 'End Call' stops all media tracks, releases the media stream, and closes the VideoCallModal.
No unhandled promise rejections or JavaScript console errors appear during the full call lifecycle.
The VideoCallModal UI matches the existing warm coral/amber theme.

constraints: Do not change any backend Motoko logic — this is a frontend-only fix.
Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
True peer-to-peer WebRTC is not available; the remote video area must remain a simulated placeholder.
nonGoals: Real peer-to-peer WebRTC signalling or actual remote video streams.
Changes to public chat, friends panel search, friend requests, or private chat messaging.
Backend schema or Motoko actor changes.
